mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 65% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting corporate revenue expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and higher corporate profit margins, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 direction, with an out-of-sample accuracy rate below 52% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of instances.
